register_pinned_actions.action_name: Genetic counseling and cascade testing for ATM pathogenic variant

trigger.summary: ATM c.8147T>C heterozygous pathogenic variant documented.

register_pinned_actions.guideline_anchor: ACMG Secondary Findings v3.2 / NCCN Genetic-Familial High-Risk Assessment
register_pinned_actions.intervention: Refer to cancer genetics for variant counseling, gene-specific surveillance review, and cascade testing offer to adult first-degree relatives.
register_pinned_actions.tracking_metric: Genetics visit completed; cascade offer documented for adult first-degree relatives.
register_pinned_actions.rationale: A pathogenic ATM finding is a heritable cancer-risk result with implications for the patient and relatives. The standard action is counseling and cascade testing, not LLM ranking.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: USPSTF hepatitis C virus infection screening adults 18–79, Grade B
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Order one-time HCV antibody with reflex RNA if positive, unless prior documented result exists.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: Universal adult preventive service with no result in source data.

spareq.s_reason: Untreated chronic HCV can progress to cirrhosis, hepatocellular carcinoma, liver failure, and ongoing transmission.
spareq.p_reason: Age 47 is squarely within the universal adult screening band and no prior HCV result is documented, so the patient-specific trigger is active.
spareq.a_reason: A positive screen directly redirects outcomes through confirmatory RNA testing and curative antiviral treatment; a negative result closes the one-time SoC gap.
spareq.r_reason: The action is ready and feasible at the next routine lab draw with standard antibody testing and reflex RNA workflow.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is very low: one blood test, minimal risk, and no invasive procedure unless follow-up is clinically indicated.
spareq.q_reason: The retrieved candidate citations include a direct USPSTF recommendation statement for HCV screening.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: USPSTF HIV screening adolescents and adults 15–65, Grade A
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Order HIV-1/2 antigen-antibody screen unless prior documented result exists.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: Universal adult preventive service with no result in source data.

spareq.s_reason: Undiagnosed HIV has major morbidity, mortality, and transmission implications when missed.
spareq.p_reason: Age 47 falls within the universal adult screening range and no prior HIV result is documented, so the patient-specific trigger is active.
spareq.a_reason: A positive screen enables confirmatory diagnosis, antiretroviral therapy, transmission reduction, and linkage to longitudinal HIV care; a negative result closes the SoC gap.
spareq.r_reason: The action is highly feasible as a standard HIV-1/2 antigen-antibody blood test bundled with routine labs.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is very low: a single blood test with minimal physical risk and clear follow-up pathways.
spareq.q_reason: The guideline anchor is strong, but the retrieved citations for this candidate are secondary/indirect or weak rather than a direct USPSTF HIV recommendation statement.

trigger.summary: Age 47; no prior colorectal screening documented.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: USPSTF colorectal cancer screening adults 45–75, Grade A/B
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Complete colorectal cancer screening now. Colonoscopy is preferred if family-history clarification may change interval; stool-based options acceptable if average-risk pathway chosen.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: CRC screening completed; modality and next interval documented.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: Age alone triggers CRC screening. ATM may affect risk discussion, but the guideline-safe trigger is universal age 45+ screening unless confirmed syndrome-specific criteria apply.

spareq.s_reason: Colorectal cancer screening addresses cancer prevention and cancer-death prevention, including detection/removal of precancerous lesions or earlier-stage cancer.
spareq.p_reason: Age 47 meets universal CRC screening criteria and no prior colorectal screening is documented, so this patient truly triggers the action.
spareq.a_reason: Screening is outcome-redirecting and stage-shifting: colonoscopy can remove premalignant lesions, and stool-based or endoscopic strategies can detect cancer earlier.
spareq.r_reason: Multiple standard pathways are ready now; colonoscopy is feasible if family-history clarification may affect interval, while stool-based testing remains an acceptable average-risk option.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is low-to-moderate rather than minimal because colonoscopy requires preparation/sedation/logistics and stool testing requires completion and follow-up if positive.
spareq.q_reason: The guideline anchor is direct standard-of-care, but the retrieved candidate citations are implementation/rationale literature rather than a direct USPSTF recommendation statement.

soc_ranked_candidates.action_name: Baseline PSA discussion and test for ATM carrier

trigger.summary: Male, age 47, ATM pathogenic variant; no PSA value documented.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: NCCN Prostate Cancer Early Detection high-risk germline pathway / Philadelphia consensus
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Shared-decision discussion and baseline serum PSA now; set follow-up cadence from result and high-risk pathway.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: PSA value documented; repeat interval or urology threshold recorded.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: High-risk germline prostate pathways begin earlier than population-average PSA discussions. This is a true missed SoC candidate in the prior run.

spareq.s_reason: The action addresses potentially lethal prostate cancer in a germline ATM carrier, where earlier detection can materially change stage at diagnosis.
spareq.p_reason: Male sex, age 47, documented pathogenic ATM variant, and no PSA value documented make the patient-specific high-risk screening trigger active.
spareq.a_reason: Baseline PSA and shared decision-making can trajectory-anchor future surveillance and may stage-shift prostate cancer if detected, but benefits depend on result, interval, and downstream evaluation.
spareq.r_reason: The next step is readily feasible: a serum PSA plus documented shared-decision discussion and follow-up threshold/cadence.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is modest because PSA testing is simple, but false positives, anxiety, urology referral, MRI/biopsy, and preference-sensitive tradeoffs are possible.
spareq.q_reason: The retrieved citations are secondary consensus/review/rationale literature rather than a direct NCCN early-detection pathway citation.

soc_ranked_candidates.action_name: Seasonal influenza and current COVID vaccination status check

trigger.summary: Seasonal influenza/COVID vaccination status not documented.

soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Verify current-season influenza and current COVID vaccination; vaccinate if due.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: Routine adult preventive care item absent from source data.

spareq.s_reason: Seasonal influenza and COVID vaccination reduce clinically important respiratory morbidity and, less commonly in a healthy 47-year-old, severe outcomes.
spareq.p_reason: Vaccination status is undocumented, but true due status depends on the current season, current CDC formulation, and prior vaccination timing.
spareq.a_reason: Vaccination can prevent infection severity and complications if due, but it is a seasonal risk-reduction action rather than a diagnostic stage-shift.
spareq.r_reason: Verification and administration are highly feasible through primary care or pharmacy workflows.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is very low: brief status check and vaccination if due, with typical transient vaccine side effects.
spareq.q_reason: The retrieved candidate citations include a direct ACIP adult immunization schedule citation.

soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Verify HepB vaccine series or immunity; vaccinate if incomplete/nonimmune.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: Universal adult immunization rule; not a risk-model insight, but true SoC.

spareq.s_reason: HBV vaccination prevents hepatitis B infection and downstream chronic hepatitis, cirrhosis, and hepatocellular carcinoma.
spareq.p_reason: Age 47 falls within the ACIP universal adult HepB vaccination recommendation and vaccination/immunity status is undocumented.
spareq.a_reason: Completing vaccination prevents future HBV infection and anchors immunization status, though there is no current infection or exposure event documented.
spareq.r_reason: Vaccine-history review, immunity testing if chosen, and starting/completing vaccination are routine and feasible at a preventive visit or pharmacy.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is low: vaccination series logistics and typical minor vaccine side effects.
spareq.q_reason: The standard-of-care anchor is clear, but the retrieved citations for this candidate are weak or not directly about universal adult HepB vaccination.

soc_ranked_candidates.action_name: Clarify paternal GI cancer primary site before pancreatic-surveillance pin

trigger.summary: Family history says paternal grandfather “GI cancer” but does not document pancreatic primary.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: NCCN/CAPS high-risk pancreatic surveillance requires mutation-specific family-history details
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Obtain structured three-generation family history: cancer primary site, age at diagnosis, lineage, and pathology if available.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: Family-history primary site documented as pancreatic vs non-pancreatic GI; pancreas surveillance eligibility recomputed.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: The prior prototype pinned pancreatic surveillance from an ambiguous GI-cancer note. The real method should not convert unspecified GI cancer into PDAC eligibility without confirmation.

spareq.s_reason: If the paternal GI cancer was pancreatic cancer, the downstream outcome addressed is high-severity pancreatic cancer risk and surveillance eligibility.
spareq.p_reason: The family-history data gap is real and actionable, but current source data only says paternal grandfather “GI cancer” and does not confirm pancreatic primary.
spareq.a_reason: Structured family-history clarification is a trajectory-anchor: it can prevent inappropriate pancreatic surveillance and can also unlock correct high-risk surveillance if PDAC is confirmed.
spareq.r_reason: The action is feasible during the visit, but may require family contact or records/pathology to resolve the primary site with confidence.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is modest: history-taking and possible records retrieval, without immediate invasive testing or pancreatic surveillance being ordered.
spareq.q_reason: The retrieved citations are secondary review/rationale literature about pancreatic surveillance criteria rather than a direct NCCN/CAPS guideline source.

soc_ranked_candidates.action_name: Confirm kidney status with cystatin C eGFR and urine ACR

trigger.summary: eGFRcr 72 with creatinine 1.25; cystatin C and urine ACR not documented.

soc_ranked_candidates.guideline_anchor: KDIGO 2024 CKD evaluation: confirm GFR when creatinine may be biased; assess albuminuria
soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Order serum cystatin C for eGFRcr-cys and urine albumin-creatinine ratio.
soc_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: eGFRcr-cys and UACR documented; CKD category confirmed or removed.
soc_ranked_candidates.rationale: Creatinine-based eGFR can be biased by muscle mass. UACR is required to stage kidney risk; cystatin C resolves whether eGFRcr 72 is true G2 or phenotype artifact.

spareq.s_reason: Incorrect kidney-risk classification can affect long-term CKD monitoring, cardiovascular/kidney risk assessment, and medication decisions.
spareq.p_reason: eGFRcr 72 with creatinine 1.25 and missing UACR/cystatin C creates a plausible patient-specific staging uncertainty, though true CKD is not established.
spareq.a_reason: Cystatin C eGFR and urine ACR are trajectory-anchoring diagnostics that can confirm, reclassify, or remove CKD risk status and set monitoring intensity.
spareq.r_reason: Both tests are ready and feasible as standard serum/urine labs at the next draw.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is very low: one blood test and one urine sample with minimal risk.
spareq.q_reason: The retrieved citations provide secondary KDIGO/rationale support but not a clean direct KDIGO guideline citation.

soc_ranked_candidates.intervention: Verify Tdap history and Td/Tdap booster date; vaccinate if no adult Tdap or booster overdue.

spareq.s_reason: Tdap/Td maintenance prevents tetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis, but near-term severity for this patient is lower absent wound/exposure or special risk context.
spareq.p_reason: Tdap/Td status is undocumented, so the gap may be active, but true due status depends on prior adult Tdap and the last booster date.
spareq.a_reason: A booster closes a preventive-care gap and reduces future infectious risk, but it is less outcome-redirecting or stage-shifting than cancer screening or major infection detection.
spareq.r_reason: Verification and booster if due are highly feasible in routine primary-care or pharmacy workflows.
spareq.e_reason: Expected burden is very low: brief status check and vaccination if due, with typical transient vaccine side effects.
spareq.q_reason: The retrieved candidate citations include a direct ACIP/MMWR recommendation citation.

considered_and_excluded.reason: Not automatically eligible: age 47, CAC=0, normotensive, A1c 5.3, Lp(a) 72 nmol/L optimal, estimated 10-year ASCVD ~low. Revisit if risk estimate/risk enhancers change.

considered_and_excluded.action_name: hs-CRP as a standalone SoC action
considered_and_excluded.reason: ACC/AHA uses hs-CRP as a risk-enhancer when statin decision is uncertain in borderline/intermediate risk. Current CAC=0 and very-low estimated risk make EVOI low; can be bundled with future lipids but should not rank above true universal gaps.
Not pinned from current source data because family history documents “GI cancer,” not confirmed pancreatic cancer. Needs family-history clarification first.

method_notes: Corrected SPAREQ semantics: S=severity, P=patient-specific priority/likelihood trigger is real, A=outcome-redirect/stage-shift/trajectory-anchor actionability, R=readiness/feasibility, E=expected harm/burden with 4=lowest burden, Q=evidence/guideline quality.
Scored only candidates present in the deterministic grounded register; no ranked candidates were invented or removed.
SOC-GEN-ATM-CASCADE was kept as mandatory=true in register_pinned_actions rather than ranked.

Ambiguous paternal grandfather GI cancer was not converted to confirmed pancreatic cancer; pancreatic surveillance remains excluded pending family-history clarification, and SOC-CANCER-FH-CLARIFY-PDAC remains a ranked diagnostic/data-quality action.
Lp(a) retained as 72 nmol/L in exclusions/risk context; no alternate-unit conversion was made.

Citation quality labels use only citations already present in each candidate record; weak labels reflect irrelevant or indirect retrieved citations rather than a judgment that the guideline anchor is false.
Weighted score formula: S*0.25 + P*0.20 + A*0.20 + R*0.15 + E*0.10 + Q*0.10, with integer dimensions 1-4.
